m_Item1.QuestionContext:
Following table describes flexible price of tickets between two stations of Indian railways and number of tickets sold during four months. Read the data and answer the questions that follow.

Premium ClassFirst ClassSecond Class
MonthNumber of tickets soldPrice per ticket in Rs.Number of tickets soldPrice per ticket in Rs.Number of tickets soldPrice per ticket in Rs.
April 20235008005005001000200
May 2023800100010004002000500
Jun 20236007008002001500300
Jul 2023500900300300900100

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which month registered the least revenue collection in first class category?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Revenue collection is number of tickets sold * price per ticket. By observation we can see that the least revenue is collection in first class category in July.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which class registered the highest revenue collection in the month of Jun 2023?
SubQuestions.Explanation: The revenue collection in the month of June 2023 was registered in second class (Rs 4500)

SubQuestions.QuestionText: By what percent collection received in Jul 2023 in second class category is less than that received in Jun 2023 in same class?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Collection received in Jul 2023 in second class category = 900*100 = 90000. Collection received in June 2023 in the same category = 1500*300 = 450000. Hence the answer is 360000/450000 = 0.8 i.e. 80%.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: What is the percent decrease in total sales of tickets, combining all classes, during the given period?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
We need to calculate total sales from all classes in the month of April and July only.
Total sales of tickets in April = 500+500+1000 = 2000 and that in July = 500+300+900 = 1700. Hence the percent decrease in sales of tickets over the given period is 300/2000 = 0.15 = 15%.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: If in Aug 2023, price of tickets are increased by 10% in each class and number of tickets sold are also expected to increase by 50 in each class, what is the percent increase in the collection from July 2023 to Aug 2023?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
Lets tabulate the rates and tickets sold for July and August from given information.

Premium ClassFirst ClassSecond Class
MonthNumber of tickets soldPrice per ticket in Rs.Number of tickets soldPrice per ticket in Rs.Number of tickets soldPrice per ticket in Rs.
Jul 2023500900300300900100
Aug 2023550990350330950110
  
Revenue collection in July was 450000+90000+90000 = 630000.
Revenue collection in Aug would be 544500+115500+104500 = 764500.
Hence, percent increase in the collection will be 134500/630000 = 21.3%

m_Item1.QuestionContext: Directions for 6 to 10: Following bar graph shows number of test and ODI centuries scored by Virat Kohli against various oppositions mentioned on X-axis. Study the graph and answer the questions that follow.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: What is the difference between ODI centuries scored by Virat against Australia and England?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Virat scored 8 ODI centuries against Australia and 3 ODI centuries against England. Hence the difference is 5.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: What is the difference between Test centuries scored by Virat against West Indies and Sri Lanka?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Virat scored 5 Test centuries against Sri Lanka and 3 test centuries against West Indies. Hence the difference is 2.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Against how many oppositions Virat scored equal number of test centuries and ODI centuries?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Virat scored 8 test centuries and 8 ODI centuries against Australia.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Number of test centuries as a percentage of number of ODI centuries is the least for which of the following opposition?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Virat scored zero test centuries against Pakistan and Zimbabwe. Hence Number of test centuries as a percentage of number of ODI centuries is 0% against them.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: Which of the following is true?

Options.OptionText: Sri Lanka, discarding Pakistan and Zimbabwe, is the only opposition against which the percentage of test centuries out of total centuries scored by Virat is the lowest.
Bangladesh, discarding Pakistan and Zimbabwe, is the only opposition against which the percentage of ODI centuries out of total centuries scored by Virat is the highest.
Virat scored more ODI centuries against West Indies, New Zealand and South Africa taken together than Australia and Sri Lanka taken together.
Virat scored more Test centuries against England, Sri Lanka and Bangladesh taken together than New Zealand, West Indies and South Africa taken together.

SubQuestions.Explanation:
Option A: Sri Lanka and Bangladesh are the two oppositions, against which the percentage of test centuries out of total centuries scored by Virat is the lowest. So this option is not true.
Option B: Sri Lanka and Bangladesh are the two oppositions against which the percentage of ODI centuries out of total centuries scored by Virat is the lowest. So this option is not true.
Option C: Virat scored 18 centuries against West Indies, New Zealand and South Africa together. He scored 18 centuries against Australia and Sri Lanka together. So this option is not true.
Hence option D is true.
Option D: He scored 12 centuries against England, Sri Lanka and Bangladesh together. He scored 9 centuries against West Indies, New Zealand and South Africa together.

m_Item1.QuestionContext: The following cumulative line graph describes number of mobile handsets sold at an outlet. The number shown at each month indicates the total number of handsets sold till and including that month. Study the graph carefully and answer the questions that follow.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: How many mobileshandsets were sold in the month of July?
By what percent, number of mobiles handsets sold in the month of September is more/less than that sold in the month of December?
What percent of total handsets were sold in the months of Mar and April together?
For how many months, number of handsets sold were equal and what was the count?
SubQuestions.Explanation: 4 months, Mar, May, Oct and Dec

SubQuestions.QuestionText: In how many months, number of handsets sold were less than the average number of handsets sold in all months together?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Average number of handsets sold = 150. For months Jan, Jun, Jul, Aug and Nov less than 150 handsets were sold.

m_Item1.QuestionContext:
Following bar graph describes the revenue and expenditure of a company named ABC ltd over 5 years. The pie chart tells about details of expenditure of the same company for the year 2019. Study the graphs carefully and answer the questions that follow.
​

​

SubQuestions.QuestionText: What is the average expenses generated over all the years?
SubQuestions.Explanation:
Find the average = sum /number of years
                            = (10 +15+18 + 20 +25)/5 lakhs  =1760000

SubQuestions.QuestionText: In which year profit percent was the highest? (Profit percent = (Profit/revenue)*100)
SubQuestions.Explanation: 66.66% profit in 2015.

SubQuestions.QuestionText: What is the average annual growth rate of revenue over the years?
SubQuestions.Explanation: AAGR(average annual growth rate) = sum of growth rates/number of period

SubQuestions.QuestionText: What was the amount spent on operations in the year 2019 (in )?

If in the year 2020, Admin, Marketing and Sales expenses go up by 10% each, whereas the remaining expenses remain unchanged, by what percent total expenses would go up in 2020 as compared to that in 2019?
SubQuestions.Explanation: Increase in expenses is 15000 on 25L.
